summary: A single large rectangular two-part structure with an inner courtyard and two tall square towers at opposite corners connected by a curved stepped red-tiled roof. Exterior walls are banded horizontally with a pale yellow fill and thick white vertical/horizontal trim; white low fence/bollards surround the base on a dirt floor/plot. The roof is predominantly red brick (stair/step pattern) with stone/white cap courses and small light blocks atop towers.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main mass with inner open courtyard; curved pitched roof connecting two towers
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along axis between towers, but curved roof breaks perfect symmetry

elements: curved stepped red-tiled roof connecting towers
two tall square towers with overhanging cap roofs
banded pale-yellow walls with thick white horizontal and vertical trim
single central ground-level entrance on long side
small square windows on tower shafts
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular outer wall ring and subtract an inner rectangular courtyard to create an L-shaped footprint as seen from top-down.
Use sandstone (or similar pale yellow) for main wall courses and place 2-3 block-high white quartz bands at regular vertical intervals; add vertical white pilasters at corners and midpoints.

Build two identical square towers rising above the roof at opposite ends of the courtyard; top each tower with an overhanging layered cap using stone/stone slabs and a flat red-brick slab roof with a central sea-lantern or light.
Construct a continuous curved stepped roof between towers using brick stairs/blocks in a stepped slope; cap roof edges with white coping blocks.
Place small 1x1 windows regularly up the tower faces and a single recessed doorway centered on the long exterior face.
Surround building base with spaced white bollard blocks (approx every 2-3 blocks) on the dirt plot.
uncertainties: Exact material IDs: pale yellow wall appears to be sandstone or smooth_sandstone; white trim likely quartz but could be white concrete.
Interior floor plan and number of internal floors are not visible; '2 floors' is an estimate from wall height and window placement.
Precise dimensions and spacing of bands, pilasters and bollards are estimated from perspective images and top-down view; offsets may vary by ±2-4 blocks.
Tower cap materials and small rooftop lights are uncertain (could be sea lanterns or different light blocks).
